mechanism_narrative: Consistent growth in retail sales indicates robust consumer demand and spending, which directly translates to higher corporate revenues and earnings, thereby driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over a one-month horizon.
evidence_refs: fact:consumer spending is a major component of GDP
failure_condition: SPX 20d returns are not positively correlated with RSAFS month-over-month growth (Rank IC < 0.05) over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A significant and sustained increase in the unemployment rate signals a deteriorating labor market, leading to reduced consumer confidence and spending, which negatively impacts corporate profits and subsequently S&P 500 forward returns over a quarterly horizon.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises > 0.5 percentage points over 3 months
evidence_refs: fact:rising unemployment typically precedes economic contractions
failure_condition: SPX 60d returns remain positive or flat in more than 50% of instances where UNRATE rises by more than 0.5 percentage points over 3 months.

mechanism_narrative: When the Federal Funds Rate is elevated and inflation continues to accelerate, it indicates an aggressive monetary tightening stance by the central bank to curb price pressures. This policy dampens economic activity and increases the cost of capital, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns.
evidence_refs: fact:Fed tightening cycles often precede market downturns
failure_condition: SPX 20d returns are positive in more than 40% of periods where both FEDFUNDS > 4.0% and CPIAUCSL MoM growth > 0.3%.

mechanism_narrative: During an early-cycle economic expansion, an increase in housing starts reflects growing consumer and business confidence, stimulating construction and related industries. This broad economic activity leads to positive S&P 500 forward returns over a quarterly horizon.
evidence_refs: fact:housing starts are a leading indicator of economic health
failure_condition: In identified early-cycle expansion regimes, HOUST month-over-month growth does not correlate positively with SPX 60d returns (Rank IC < 0.03).

mechanism_narrative: Sustained growth in industrial production indicates robust manufacturing and supply-side activity, signaling healthy corporate earnings and capacity utilization. This supports positive S&P 500 forward returns over a short, weekly horizon.
failure_condition: SPX 5d returns show no significant positive correlation with INDPRO month-over-month growth over a 5-year rolling window (p-value > 0.1).

mechanism_narrative: Strong monthly job creation, as indicated by nonfarm payrolls exceeding 150,000, reflects a robust labor market. This boosts consumer confidence and spending, which in turn drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over a one-month horizon.
evidence_refs: fact:strong job growth supports economic expansion
failure_condition: SPX 20d returns are negative or flat in more than 50% of months where PAYEMS growth exceeds 150,000 jobs.

mechanism_narrative: A significant reduction in the federal funds rate signals an easing monetary policy, which lowers borrowing costs for businesses and consumers. This stimulates economic activity and supports positive S&P 500 forward returns over a quarterly horizon.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS declines > 0.25 percentage points over 3 months
evidence_refs: fact:Fed rate cuts typically aim to stimulate the economy
failure_condition: During periods of FEDFUNDS decline > 0.25 percentage points over 3 months, SPX 60d returns are not significantly positive (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A combination of negative GDP growth and persistent inflation indicates a stagflationary environment, where economic contraction is coupled with rising prices. This severely dampens corporate profits and consumer purchasing power, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 quarterly growth < 0% AND CPIAUCSL month-over-month growth > 0.2%
evidence_refs: fact:stagflation is historically detrimental to equity markets
failure_condition: SPX 20d returns are positive in more than 30% of periods exhibiting negative GDPC1 growth and positive CPIAUCSL MoM growth.
